steps.observed: Dashboard loaded successfully with 'Mission Board' heading and Kanban board layout

steps.expected: Columns: backlog, ready, in_progress, review, done, blocked
steps.observed: All 6 columns confirmed: Backlog (h3), Ready (h3), In Progress (h3), Review (h3), Done (h3), Blocked (h3). Each column header has a task count badge.
No console errors

steps.expected: Cards show title, status, agent name (or Unclaimed), task type
steps.observed: Cards display: title (e.g. 'Backlog task one'), task type badge (Feature, Bugfix, Implementation), agent name (UUID or 'Unclaimed'). Format: '<title> <type> <agent>'

steps.expected: Cards positioned in columns matching their status
steps.observed: Verified: Backlog column contains only backlog tasks, Ready column contains ready tasks, In Progress contains in_progress tasks, etc. All cards correctly placed.
Annotated screenshot captured showing all task cards with proper layout

steps.action: Select 'Beta Project' from project filter dropdown
steps.observed: Dashboard filtered to show only Beta Project tasks: 'Implement search feature' (backlog), 'Write API documentation' (in_progress), 'Review task' (review), 'Blocked task' (blocked). Verified against API data for project_id=456c1c8b.
Dashboard filtered to show only Alpha Project tasks. Confirmed correct project filtering by cross-referencing API response.

steps.expected: Project dropdown shows all available projects
steps.observed: Dropdown shows: All Projects, Alpha Project, Beta Project, Alpha Project (duplicate project in DB)

steps.expected: Only backlog tasks shown across all columns
steps.observed: All 3 backlog tasks displayed. Other columns (Ready, In Progress, Review, Done, Blocked) showed 0 tasks each.

steps.action: Add agent filter 'aaaaaaaa-aaaa-aaaa-aaaa-aaaaaaaaaaaa' on top of status filter
steps.expected: Combined filter shows only backlog tasks assigned to that agent
steps.observed: 'No matching tasks' displayed since no backlog tasks were assigned to that agent. Both filters working together correctly.

steps.action: Clear all filters via 'Clear all filters' button
steps.observed: All tasks reappeared after clearing filters
Only 'Restore task 1' and 'Restore task 2' displayed in Backlog column. Other task 'val-form-created-task' filtered out.
'No matching tasks' heading with 'Clear all filters' button displayed

steps.action: Clear search via 'Clear all filters' button
steps.observed: All tasks reappeared after clearing

steps.action: Click 'Restore task 1' card in Backlog column
steps.observed: Dialog opened with role='dialog' and aria-label='Task details: Restore task 1'

steps.expected: Full description, timestamps, agent info, activity history shown
steps.observed: Detail view contains: Title, Status badge ('Backlog'), Task type ('implementation'), Description ('Seed restore'), Project ('Alpha Project'), Assigned Agent ('Unclaimed'), Created timestamp, Updated timestamp, Claimed timestamp ('—'), Task ID, Activity History section with 'Created' entry and timestamp
Close button (×), Edit button, and Delete button all present in detail view

evidence.network: Dialog rendered client-side from already-fetched task data

steps.observed: Network route configured successfully

steps.action: Reload page and check for loading indicator
steps.expected: Loading state visible while data fetching
steps.observed: Loading state element found (data-testid='loading-state') displaying 'Loading board...' text with spinning animation (.board-spinner). Loading state confirmed present during API fetch delay.

steps.expected: Screenshot captured showing spinner and loading text
steps.observed: Screenshot captured showing centered loading indicator with spinner and 'Loading board...' message on dark background

frictions.description: Seeded data was modified by other subagents running in parallel (tasks created/deleted), requiring multiple reloads and API verification during testing. Initial test data (12 tasks across 6 statuses) was reduced to 3 backlog-only tasks partway through testing.
frictions.resolution: Adapted test approach to verify filter mechanisms work correctly with available data. Used curl API calls to cross-reference dashboard state. Verified filtering logic with both matching and non-matching queries.

frictions.description: API server became temporarily unavailable during testing (likely restarted by another subagent), causing dashboard to show error state. Required waiting for API recovery and clicking 'Retry connection' button.
frictions.resolution: Waited ~15 seconds for API to recover, then clicked 'Retry connection' button. Dashboard successfully reconnected and resumed normal operation.

frictions.description: Duplicate 'Alpha Project' entries in project dropdown due to two projects sharing the same name in the database (different UUIDs). One project had no tasks.
frictions.resolution: Used JavaScript eval to programmatically select the correct project by value (UUID) to ensure testing the project with actual tasks.

summary: Tested 7 assertions: 7 passed. All dashboard read/filter/detail features working correctly: Kanban board loads with 6 status columns (VAL-DASH-001), task cards display title/type/agent (VAL-DASH-002), project filter works (VAL-DASH-004), status and agent filters work independently and together (VAL-DASH-005), search filters tasks in real-time (VAL-DASH-006), task detail view shows comprehensive information (VAL-DASH-008), and loading state displays spinner with message (VAL-DASH-010). No console errors throughout testing. Note: seeded data was modified by parallel subagent operations but all UI mechanisms verified working.
